description: The partner code of the loan
The unique identifier of the loan
The schedule number of the repayment
The date of the repayment schedule
The principal amount to be repaid
The interest amount to be repaid
The additional interest amount to be repaid
The penal interest amount to be repaid
The expected principal balance after the repayment
The overdue equated monthly installment (EMI) amount
The unique code or identifier of the partner associated with the loan collection
The unique identifier of the loan account for which the collection is being processed
The date on which the loan collection repayment is scheduled
The date of demand for the loan collection
The unique identifier of the loan collection transaction
The type of loan repayment being collected (e.g., installment, full payment, partial payment, etc.)
The amount of excess payment made by the borrower, which is the additional payment made on top of the regular loan installment
The mode or method of loan repayment used by the borrower (e.g., cash, check, online payment, etc.)

The reference or identification number associated with the loan collection transaction. This reference number can be used for tracking and reconciling the transaction with other related records or systems
The unique identifier of the original loan collection transaction to which the current transaction relates. This is useful when there are changes or updates to a previous transaction
The amount of the loan principal collected from the borrower during the loan collection process.
The amount of interest collected from the borrower during the loan collection process
The additional interest amount collected from the borrower (e.g., late payment fees, penalties, etc.)
The amount of penal interest collected from the borrower during the loan collection process. Penal interest is charged as a penalty for delayed or missed payments
The amount of fees collected from the borrower during the loan collection process. These fees may include processing fees, service charges, etc
The amount of unapplied credits collected from the borrower. Unapplied credits are payments that have not been applied to any specific loan account or invoice
The total amount collected from the borrower during the loan collection process, including principal, interest, penalties, fees, etc
The amount of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) deducted from the collected amount as per applicable tax regulations
The amount of loan principal waived during the loan collection process. Principal waiver refers to the forgiveness of a portion of the loan amount by the lender
The amount of interest waived during the loan collection process. Interest waiver refers to the forgiveness of a portion of the interest amount by the lender
The amount of additional interest waived during the loan collection process. This includes any extra interest charges that have been waived by the lender
The amount of penal interest waived during the loan collection process. This includes any penalty interest charges that have been forgiven by the lender
The amount of fees waived during the loan collection process. This includes any processing fees or service charges that have been forgiven by the lender
The user or personnel responsible for processing the loan collection transaction
The date of the loan collection transaction
The time of the loan collection transaction
